metadata.title: Environmental Reporting Obligation Rules
metadata.note: Federal timelines only. State permits may impose shorter deadlines — always check your specific permit.

rules.condition: Emission limit exceedance (CEMS rolling average exceeds NESHAP limit)
rules.federal_timeline: Report excess emissions within 2 business days per §63.10(d)(5). Include in semi-annual deviation report.
rules.message: EMISSION LIMIT EXCEEDANCE — Report to permitting authority within 2 business days. Document: pollutant, duration, magnitude, cause, corrective action.

rules.federal_timeline: Report in next semi-annual compliance report. Implement corrective maintenance immediately.
rules.message: CEMS DATA AVAILABILITY WARNING — Below 90% threshold. Substitute data rules apply. Prioritize CEMS repair. Document all downtime periods and reasons.

rules.federal_timeline: Emergency — document immediately. Include in semi-annual report. Extended outage may require notification to permitting authority.
rules.message: CEMS DATA AVAILABILITY EMERGENCY — Below 75%. All data during outage is substitute data. Facility compliance status is at risk. Emergency repair required.

rules.federal_timeline: Data invalid back to last successful RATA. Substitute data applies for entire period. Report in semi-annual compliance report. Schedule retest within 30 days.
rules.message: FAILED RATA — All CEMS data since last successful RATA is invalid. Substitute data procedures apply. Schedule retest. Quantify compliance exposure for the invalid data period.

rules.federal_timeline: Log duration and reason in real time. Report all bypass events in semi-annual compliance report per §63.1348(b).
rules.message: BYPASS STACK EVENT — Log: start time, end time, duration, reason, estimated emissions during bypass. Bypass emissions are NOT exempt from compliance determination.

rules.federal_timeline: Report to permitting authority within 2 days per §63.1344(e). Document for affirmative defense immediately.
rules.message: MALFUNCTION WITH EMISSIONS — No malfunction exemption under current rule. Report within 2 days. Begin documenting for affirmative defense: maintenance records, operating logs, actions taken to minimize emissions, root cause analysis.

rules.federal_timeline: 60-day retest required. Report results to state permitting agency per §63.1349(b).
rules.message: STACK TEST FAILURE — Schedule retest within 60 days. Report results to permitting authority. Review process conditions at time of test. Consider operational adjustments before retest.

rules.federal_timeline: Respond within permit deadline, typically 15-30 days. Engage legal counsel for significant enforcement.
rules.message: NOV RECEIVED — Acknowledge receipt. Assess severity. Prepare response within deadline. Gather documentation: compliance history, corrective actions taken, maintenance records. Engage legal counsel if penalties are significant.

rules.federal_timeline: Document immediately. Opacity uses 6-minute averages — any single exceedance above 20% is reportable per permit. Report per state-specific requirements.
rules.message: OPACITY EXCEEDANCE — Unlike other pollutants, opacity compliance is based on 6-minute averages, NOT 30-day rolling. Any single 6-minute average above 20% is a deviation. Document immediately and report per permit.

rules.federal_timeline: Report within 24 hours to EPA RCRA and state agency per 40 CFR 266.
rules.authority: EPA RCRA + State hazardous waste agency
rules.message: HAZARDOUS WASTE FUEL DEVIATION — Report within 24 hours. BIF rules impose stricter requirements than standard NESHAP. Ensure trial burn conditions were maintained. Document feed rates, emission controls, and operating parameters.

modifiers.name: during_startup
modifiers.effect: Add to response: 'Document work practices followed during startup per §63.1346. No blanket startup exemption exists under current NESHAP Subpart LLL.'

modifiers.name: bypass_open
modifiers.effect: Add to response: 'Log bypass duration and reason per §63.1348(b). Include bypass event in semi-annual compliance report. Bypass emissions are uncontrolled and must be included in excess emissions calculations.'

modifiers.name: mercury_specific
modifiers.effect: Escalate: 'Mercury exceedance requires notification to EPA Region within 24 hours in addition to state reporting.'
